request.question: What items could you find delivered to Trout Creek, Utah?

title: Pony Express
Trout Creek, Utah
Gauff Hill, Pennsylvania
Sheepheaven Creek redband trout
Trout River (Iowa)
Ibapah Peak
Trout Creek, Montana
Alvord cutthroat trout
Arkansas Hills
Golden trout
sentences: The Pony Express was a mail service delivering messages, newspapers, and mail.
Trout Creek is a small farming unincorporated community, in western Juab County, Utah, United States.
It is located along the Pony Express/Overland route in northern Snake Valley, north of Partoun and south of Callao.
It is named after the creek that flows from the west off of the Deep Creek Mountains.
It has one of the most remote Mormon chapels in Utah, with a short section of paved road, the only paved road for over 50 mi .
Gauff Hill is an unincorporated community in extreme eastern Salisbury Township, Lehigh County, Pennsylvania.
It is at the head of a valley between arms of South Mountain to the north and south and is separated from the Lehigh River by the former.
The East Branch Trout Creek starts just east of the village and flows west to join the South Branch in Allentown to become the Trout Creek, a tributary of the Lehigh River.
The Sheepheaven Creek redband trout is a local Californian variety of the rainbow trout ("Oncorhynchus mykiss"), a freshwater fish in the Salmonidae family.

It is considered either a distinct western form of the McCloud River redband trout (subspecies "Oncorhynchus mykiss stonei"), or a subspecies of its own, which has not been scientifically named and described yet.
It is native to Sheepheaven Creek, Siskiyou County, California, United States.
It has been transplanted into Swamp Creek in 1972 and 1974 and into Trout Creek in 1977.
They can now be found in both locations.
Sheepheaven Creek redband are found to be the most distinct among all other trout groups, and therefore has been suggested to merit recognition as a new subspecies.
They have the fewest gill rakers of any western trout.
The Trout River is a 13.5 mi tributary of the Upper Iowa River in northeastern Iowa.
It rises in Frankville Township in Winneshiek County, south of Iowa Highway 9, and flows north to join the Upper Iowa River in Glenwood Township, east of Decorah.
It is joined from the west by a major tributary, Trout Creek.
The river and creek should not be confused with another Trout Creek (and Trout Run) which flow into the Upper Iowa River 5 mi to the west at Decorah.
Ibapah Peak is a 12018 ft summit in Juab County, Utah in the United States.
It is the highest point of the Deep Creek Range and is located less than 5 mi east of the Utah-Nevada border, and about 10 mi northwest of the town of Trout Creek, Utah.
With a topographic prominence of 5247 ft it is the fifth-most prominent summit in Utah.
Trout Creek is a census-designated place (CDP) in Sanders County, Montana, United States.
The population was 261 at the 2000 census.
Trout Creek was proclaimed "Huckleberry Capital of Montana" by the state's legislature in 1981.
The Alvord cutthroat trout, "Oncorhynchus clarki alvordensis", was a subspecies of cutthroat trout.
It was known only from Trout Creek in Oregon and Virgin Creek in Nevada, although it may have lived in several of the larger Alvord Basin streams during recent times.
It was native to spring-fed creeks that ran down to Alvord Dry Lake in southeast Oregon, which was a large lake during the ice ages and an isolated drainage, part of the Great Basin today.

This is one of the two cutthroat trout taxa considered extinct because all known populations are hybridized with rainbow trout which were introduced into streams in the Alvord basin in the 1920s, resulting in cutbows.
The Arkansas Hills are a mountain range in Central Colorado located between the upper Arkansas River Valley and the upper South Platte River Valley.
This landmass is a continuation of the ridge known as the Mosquito Range north of Trout Creek Pass.
The range continues for roughly 60 miles south-southwest from Trout Creek Pass to the town of Texas Creek.
Few perennial streams flow out of the Arkansas Hills, as the area is arid.
The range is in the rain shadow of the much higher Sawatch and Sangre de Cristo Ranges to the west across the Arkansas River Valley.
The City of Salida, Colorado is the most notable municipality near the Arkansas Hills.
To the south is the town of Cotopaxi, Colorado.
To the east is the town of Guffey, Colorado.
The highest points in the Arkansas Hills tend to be between 11,000 and 12,000 feet elevation.
The California golden trout, or simply the golden trout ("Oncorhynchus mykiss aguabonita"), is a subspecies of the rainbow trout native to California.
The golden trout is native to Golden Trout Creek (tributary to the Kern River), Volcano Creek (tributary to Golden Trout Creek), and the South Fork Kern River.
It is the state fish of California.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: What items could you find delivered to Trout Creek, Utah?
hop1_docs: Mammoth Creek | Mammoth Creek is a Utah creek which flows for over 20 miles through mountains and forests from Mammoth Summit (near Brian Head, Utah), through the Mammoth Valley, to its confluence with the Sevier River (near Hatch, Utah).  The creek contains wild brown trout and hatchery rainbow trout.

Trout Creek, Utah | Trout Creek is a small farming unincorporated community, in western Juab County, Utah, United States.  It is located along the Pony Express/Overland route in northern Snake Valley, north of Partoun and south of Callao.  It is named after the creek that flows from the west off of the Deep Creek Mountains.  It has one of the most remote Mormon chapels in Utah, with a short section of paved road, the only paved road for over 50 mi .

Ibapah Peak | Ibapah Peak is a 12018 ft summit in Juab County, Utah in the United States.  It is the highest point of the Deep Creek Range and is located less than 5 mi east of the Utah-Nevada border, and about 10 mi northwest of the town of Trout Creek, Utah.  With a topographic prominence of 5247 ft it is the fifth-most prominent summit in Utah.
Find What You Love and Let It Kill You | "Find What You Love and Let It Kill You" may refer to:
Find What You Love and Let It Kill You (Jonny Craig EP) | Find What You Love and Let It Kill You (Jonny Craig EP)
Letters to Judy: What Kids Wish They Could Tell You | Letters to Judy: What Kids Wish They Could Tell You

Navajo Lake (Utah) | Navajo Lake is a small lake in northwestern Kane County in southern Utah.  It is shallow, reaching depths of only 25 feet.  Recreation includes fishing, boating, swimming, and lodging facilities.  Species of trout in Navajo Lake are brook trout, splake trout (hybrid), and rainbow trout.  Trout survival for winter is very poor with only the splake and brook trout overwintering.  There is also a dense population of Utah chub.

response.summary_1: Passage facts: "Trout Creek is a small farming unincorporated community, in western Juab County, Utah" and "It is located along the Pony Express/Overland route in northern Snake Valley" and it "has one of the most remote Mormon chapels in Utah" (these identify the place and its remoteness). Missing fact / bridge: none of the passages state what items are delivered to Trout Creek (e.g., mail, packages, supplies, newspapers, or historical Pony Express deliveries). Retrieval direction for next hop: search for pages or records about "Trout Creek, Utah" + "post office", "mail", "delivery", "packages", "supplies", or "Pony Express/Overland route deliveries" (use queries like "Trout Creek Utah post office", "deliveries to Trout Creek Utah", or newspaper/news reports mentioning deliveries) to find which items are delivered there.
response.hop2_query: Trout Creek Utah post office mail deliveries packages supplies Pony Express
hop2_docs: Trout Creek, Utah | Trout Creek is a small farming unincorporated community, in western Juab County, Utah, United States.  It is located along the Pony Express/Overland route in northern Snake Valley, north of Partoun and south of Callao.  It is named after the creek that flows from the west off of the Deep Creek Mountains.  It has one of the most remote Mormon chapels in Utah, with a short section of paved road, the only paved road for over 50 mi .

Package delivery | Package delivery or parcel delivery is the delivery of shipping containers, parcels, or high value mail as single shipments.  The service is provided by most postal systems, express mail, private courier companies, and less than truckload shipping carriers.

U.S. Special Delivery (postal service) | U.S. Special Delivery was a postal service paid for with additional postage for urgent letters and postal packets which are delivered in less time than by standard or first class mail service.  Its meaning is different and separate from express mail delivery service.  Essentially it meant that a postal packet was delivered from a post office to the addressee immediately once it arrived at the post office responsible for delivering it, rather than waiting for the next regular delivery to the addressee.

Package tracking | Package tracking or "package logging" is the process of localizing shipping containers, mail and parcel post at different points of time during sorting, warehousing, and package delivery to verify their provenance and to predict and aid delivery.

Discontinued post office | A discontinued post office or DPO is an American postal term for a post office which has been discontinued.  Some are in ghost towns, some victims of consolidation of mail service as small post offices are closed or a city expands.  The introduction of Rural Free Delivery, RFD, in 1902 led to the closure of many post offices, which peaked in 1901 at 76,945.  In the United States, which was mostly rural, mail previously had been picked up in rural areas at small local post offices, home delivery being limited to urban areas until experimentation with rural delivery began in 1890.
Pony Express | The Pony Express was a mail service delivering messages, newspapers, and mail.

Gandy, Utah | Gandy is a small farming unincorporated community in Millard County, Utah, United States, located just east of the Nevada border.  It is located in the west-central part of Snake Valley.  It is known for Gandy Warm Springs and Gandy Creek, a large spring (15-19 cfs) that comes out of the base of Spring Mountain to the west.  It stays around 81–82 degrees Fahrenheit (27–28 Celsius) year-round.  Originally known as Smithville, Gandy was renamed in 1925 after Isaac Gandy, the first ranch owner in the area back when this was a post office stop along the Pony Express/Overland Route.
response.summary_2: Because Trout Creek lies on the Pony Express/Overland route, historically the Pony Express would have delivered messages, newspapers, and mail there. The retrieved passages do not confirm whether modern mail, parcels, or courier deliveries currently serve Trout Creek.
